note: Daily scrape for Monday 2026-05-04 covering 5/1 (Friday) news flow + 5/2-5/3 weekend (typically light). Headline new lead: Fun ($72M Series A 5/1 — payments-infrastructure company emerging from stealth, customers Polymarket/Lighter/Aave, $18B+ annual transaction volume, opening Singapore office; A-tier triple-trigger pr-marketing target — combined 88). Secondary: Nura Studios (SF, emerged from stealth 4/30 with seed funding, AI animation platform Showcraft launching 5/19 — C-tier pr-marketing/media-adjacent; combined 66). Skipped this cycle: Definity ($12M Series A 4/29 — 5 days old, missed window), Kibu ($10.5M seed 4/29 — too small + missed window), Hightouch ($150M Series D 4/27 — 7 days old + already has comms function), True Anomaly $600M defense (out of scope), Aidoc-style late-stage (skip rule still in effect). No new fund-formation, legal-consulting, coaching-ops, ai-consulting, legal-freelance, or pr-freelance leads cleared the bar from the 5/1-5/4 window — Friday tends to be the lightest funding day and weekends produce no new postings. Signal refresh: Rebel Audio May 30 launch now 26 days out (urgency rising), JuliaHub Dyad 3.0 unveil event scheduled 5/19 (15 days — peak narrative window approaching), Rogo customer-roster reveal cycle peaked 5/1, Mosaic Value Partners + General Analysis press cycles closing this week, Flex Head of Comms search ongoing (fractional pitch window still open ~3-5 more weeks).

leads.name: Fun
leads.industry: B2B Fintech / Payments Infrastructure for Internet-Native Capital Markets (Crypto Onramping, Stablecoin Settlement, High-Volume Digital Platforms)
leads.location: New York, NY (HQ) + planned Singapore office (APAC expansion)
leads.fundingStage: Series A ($72M, May 1, 2026) co-led by Multicoin Capital and SignalFire, with participation from Infinity Ventures, Pharsalus Capital, and Tinder co-founder Justin Mateen as a strategic angel. Round closed in late January 2026; company emerged from stealth concurrent with the announcement. Founded 2022; first publicly disclosed funding round.
leads.source: Fortune (exclusive 5/1) + PYMNTS + Pulse 2.0 + Crypto Briefing + Business Wire + Finsmes + Finextra + citybiz (all 5/1) + Startup Fortune (5/2)
leads.notes: $72M Series A announced May 1 — co-led by Multicoin Capital + SignalFire with Infinity Ventures, Pharsalus Capital, and Tinder co-founder Justin Mateen participating. Fun emerged from stealth concurrent with the round (founded 2022, no prior public funding) — that double-trigger (stealth-exit + Series A) plus the disclosed APAC expansion (new Singapore office) gives this lead a triple-narrative setup that's textbook A-tier pr-marketing. Customer disclosure is unusually rich: Polymarket, Lighter, and Aave are named in the press release (most stealth-exits hold the customer names back for a second press cycle — Fun chose to lead with them, which signals the comms function is sophisticated AND that there may already be a pre-Series-A agency relationship to displace). Quantitative proof points: $18B+ annual transaction volume, millions of users, 100+ countries operating now. Use of capital: engineering investment + APAC expansion (Singapore) + selective infrastructure-stack acquisitions. Primary positioning angle: 'the unified payments rail for internet-native capital markets' — the comms moment is the convergence-of-crypto-and-traditional-fintech narrative, with Fun as the infrastructure layer that makes both feel native. Recommend a 90-day plant-the-flag campaign threading: (a) the post-stealth founder/origin story (4 years in stealth + $72M is already-earned credibility), (b) the Polymarket/Aave/Lighter customer thread (specific and prestigious in crypto-native press, but also crosses over into mainstream fintech press because Polymarket is a brand-name household-name asset), (c) the Singapore expansion as a 'why now / why APAC' anchor for trade press in Asia (DealStreetAsia, Tech in Asia, Nikkei Asia), (d) a forward-looking thought-leadership angle on stablecoin-as-payment-rail for B2B finance journalists at Bloomberg/WSJ/FT. Time-sensitive because: (i) Series A coverage cycles run 7-10 days max — clock started 5/1, so the high-volume window is 5/1-5/11, (ii) the Polymarket regulatory situation (election betting) is a press-amplifier that competitors like Bridge/Stripe will be on stage about at Stripe Sessions this week, (iii) APAC expansion announcements degrade quickly — the Singapore-office press needs to hit before mid-May. Realistic comms-relationship odds: STRONG. Most stealth-exits at this stage either (a) bring in a Series-A-standard agency for 6-month retainers ($25-40k/mo), or (b) hire a Head of Comms in-house. The named-customer disclosure pattern suggests they're already getting comms guidance — but a stealth-exit company is often willing to entertain alternative agency pitches in months 1-3 when they're still figuring out which lane they're in (crypto-native vs. mainstream fintech). Mark's fintech + regulated-consumer narrative experience is the right shape; lead at the CEO/co-founder level (Fortune profiled CEO Kirby Ferguson as the primary press contact). Skip ai-consulting (Fun is the vendor, not a consulting buyer). Skip pr-freelance (no Head of Comms job posting visible — they may have one filled internally already). Combined score 88 — strongest new pr-marketing lead since JuliaHub (5/1, also 88). Note: Fun's domain registration uses 'fun.xyz' per coverage; verify the canonical website before first-touch.

leads.name: Nura Studios
leads.industry: AI-Assisted Animation Platform / Episodic Content Infrastructure for Game Studios, Animation Studios, and IP Owners
leads.fundingStage: Seed (undisclosed amount, April 30, 2026) led by iGlobe Partners with participation from Fellows Fund and Shanda Ventures. Company emerged from stealth concurrent with the round announcement. Showcraft platform enters early access on May 19, 2026.
leads.source: PR Newswire (4/30) + Yahoo Finance + Pulse 2.0 + Animation World Network + Dealroom + Finsmes (5/1) + Crunchbase
leads.notes: Seed round (undisclosed) announced April 30, emerging-from-stealth + product-launch trigger on the same press release — clean 2-trigger setup. Showcraft, the AI-assisted animation platform, enters early access May 19 (15 days out). Founders Sylvio Drouin (CEO, ex-Apple, ex-Unity), Gabriele Farina (CTO), Stefano Corazza (Chairman, ex-Adobe, ex-Mixamo) — the platform-veteran founding team is press-known in animation/3D engineering circles. Differentiator vs. text-prompt AI video (Sora, Runway, Pika): Nura is built around human-created IP — game studios feed in their existing characters, environments, and assets, and Showcraft generates episodic content from there. That 'AI for IP holders, not against them' narrative has obvious appeal with animation-trade press (Animation World Network already covered) and could cross over into entertainment-trade press (Variety, Hollywood Reporter, IndieWire) at the May 19 launch. FIT CAVEATS: (a) entertainment-tech is outside Mark's core FS/Tech motion — fit score reflects this (industry 3, budget 5), (b) seed-stage with undisclosed amount means a real budget commitment to a comms retainer is uncertain, (c) Asian-VC-led round (iGlobe Singapore + Shanda Ventures Shanghai) suggests US press strategy is still being scoped — could be opportunity. Recommend: (a) lower-priority outreach behind Fun, (b) angle is 'the human-artist-centric AI animation platform' with a May 19 launch news anchor — pitch is cleanest in the 7-10 days BEFORE the launch, not after, (c) lead-touch at CEO Sylvio Drouin since he's the press-active founder. Time-sensitive: launch event 5/19, so the 5/4-5/12 window is the agency-pitch sweet spot. Skip media (Nura is vendor, not a media buyer). Skip ai-consulting (vendor). Combined score 66 — C-tier; included because the 5/19 launch creates a clean narrative window and the human-artist positioning is differentiated enough to be a winnable agency pitch.
